title: Houses for Rent in Cleveland with a Fenced Yard
metaTitle: Houses for Rent in Cleveland with a Fenced Yard
metaDescription: Looking for a house for rent in Cleveland with a fenced yard? See typical rent ranges, where fenced-yard homes are common, and how to book a showing.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent cleveland fenced yard
answerBox: Fenced yards are common in Cleveland's older single-family and duplex housing stock, especially on lots with a detached garage. Rent Finder Cleveland works with 90+ rental homes across Greater Cleveland, some with fenced yards, and every home accepts Section 8 vouchers — book a showing to ask about a specific property's yard.

sections.h2: Can you find a house for rent with a fenced yard in Cleveland?
body: Yes — a fenced yard is a common feature in Cleveland's older residential housing stock, particularly on single-family houses and smaller duplexes built on standard city lots with a detached garage along the alley. Fencing varies by property (chain-link, wood privacy, or partial yard fencing), so it's a feature to confirm on any specific listing rather than assume citywide.

We work with **90+ rental homes** across Greater Cleveland, and while not every home has a fenced yard, it's a common enough feature in our available homes that it's worth asking about directly. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and let us know a fenced yard matters to you, or [start an application](/apply) once you've found the right home.

sections.h2: Why do renters look for a fenced yard?
body: The most common reasons renters ask for a fenced yard are containing pets safely, giving children an enclosed outdoor space, or simply having private outdoor room for gardening or gatherings. Whatever the reason, the practical questions are the same: is the yard fully or partially enclosed, what's the fence material and condition, and does the property allow pets under its own policy.

items: **Full fence:** Encloses the entire yard, typically on all sides
**Partial fence:** Encloses part of the lot (e.g., back yard only)
**Fence material:** Chain-link is most common on older Cleveland lots; wood privacy fencing appears on some renovated properties
**Pet policy:** A fenced yard does not automatically mean pets are allowed — confirm the property's pet policy separately

sections.h2: What does rent look like for houses with a yard in Cleveland?
body: Rent for a house with a fenced yard follows the same market factors as any Cleveland rental — bedroom count, condition, and neighborhood — rather than a separate price tier for the yard itself. Per **Zumper's July 4, 2026 report**, Cleveland's citywide median asking rent is $1,250/mo, with 2-bedroom homes averaging around $1,100 and 3-bedroom homes around $1,350.

rows: Larger lots more likely on East/SE side

sections.h2: Where are fenced-yard homes typically found in Cleveland?
body: Fenced yards are most common on standard single-family lots throughout Cleveland's East and Southeast side neighborhoods, where most of Rent Finder Cleveland's managed inventory sits — including **Slavic Village (44105)**, **Collinwood (44110)**, **Glenville (44108)**, **Fairfax and Central (44104)**, **Hough (44103)**, and **Buckeye-Shaker (44120)** — as well as a smaller number of West-side homes near **Cudell/Detroit-Shoreway (44102)** and **Old Brooklyn (44109)**. These neighborhoods largely consist of early-to-mid-20th-century housing with detached garages set off rear alleys, a layout that lends itself to enclosed back yards.

For more on rent and transit by area, see our [Cleveland neighborhoods hub](/cleveland-rentals/neighborhoods/), or explore our full [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/) pillar page.

sections.h2: Do fenced-yard houses accept Section 8 vouchers?
body: Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with — regardless of yard or lot type — **accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is HUD-inspection-ready**. Voucher holders should know that Ohio has no statewide source-of-income protection law and the City of Cleveland does not require landlords to accept vouchers; only a handful of suburbs (Cleveland Heights, South Euclid, University Heights, Warrensville Heights, and Linndale) have local ordinances protecting voucher holders. Learn more on the [CMHA Housing Choice Voucher page](https://www.cmha.net/housing/applicants/housing_choice_vouchers.php) or our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/).

sections.h2: How to find a house with a fenced yard
body: Because a fenced yard is a per-property feature rather than a citywide guarantee, the fastest way to find one is to tell us directly what you're looking for. [Book a showing](/p/book-showing) and mention the yard is a priority, and we'll point you toward current listings that match.

faq.q: Are there houses for rent in Cleveland with a fenced yard?
faq.a: Yes, a fenced yard is a common feature on Cleveland's older single-family and duplex housing stock, especially properties with a detached garage. Fencing type and yard size vary by property, so it's best to ask about a specific listing. Book a showing and mention a fenced yard is a priority.

faq.q: Does a fenced yard mean pets are allowed?
faq.a: Not automatically. A fenced yard and a pet policy are separate features of a rental — some fenced-yard homes do allow pets and others don't. Always confirm the specific pet policy for a listing rather than assuming a fenced yard implies pets are welcome.

faq.q: Do fenced-yard homes cost more to rent in Cleveland?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Rent is driven mainly by bedroom count, condition, and neighborhood rather than yard fencing specifically. A fenced yard is common enough on standard Cleveland lots that it doesn't typically carry a separate price premium on its own.

faq.q: Which Cleveland neighborhoods have more houses with fenced yards?
faq.a: Fenced yards are common throughout Cleveland's East and Southeast side neighborhoods — including Slavic Village, Collinwood, Glenville, Fairfax/Central, Hough, and Buckeye-Shaker — where standard single-family lots with detached garages are the norm, as well as a smaller number of West-side homes.

faq.q: Do fenced-yard houses accept Section 8 vouchers?
faq.a: Yes. Every home Rent Finder Cleveland works with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of yard type. Voucher holders should confirm current CMHA rules and know that source-of-income protections only apply in a few Cuyahoga County suburbs, not the City of Cleveland itself.
